quoteFirst: On the 11th, at 6:00 p. m., she is 1° 15′ north of Neptune; on the 14th, at
quoteLast: , 3° 47′ north of the moon; motion direct, amounting to 39° 15′ 47″. On the 1st, she rises at 5:05 a. m., and sets at 6:45 p. m.; and on the 16th, rises at 4:59 a. m., sets at 7:21 p. m.

quoteFirst: 4 kilos was fed on July 6, at 1.10 p.m., with 640 c.c. of fluid, representing the extract of 2,000 grams of _Astragalus mollissimus_. At
quoteLast: the animal could be turned on its back, and it regained its feet with difficulty. At 1.24 p. m. it urinated and had a stool. The lamb died during the night.
title: Barium: A Cause of the Loco-Weed Disease

quoteLast: in the morning) Lieutenant Bronsfield was preparing to leave the watch and return to his cabin, when his attention was attracted by a distant hissing noise. His comrades and himself first thought that this hissing was caused by the letting off of steam; but lifting their heads, they found that the noise was produced in the highest regions of the air.
title: From the Earth to the moon; and, round the moon
author: Verne, Jules, 1828-1905
